title: How LLMs Work — Tokens, Training, and Inference
verdict: PASS — all five checks clear; one soft formatting finding (diagram reference points to ./diagram.mmd source file instead of rendered diagram.png, line 27) does not escalate to hard fail.

check_0_format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# How LLMs Work — Tokens, Training, and Inference'. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and valid. Exactly 6 fixed sections in correct order.

check_1_length.notes: 1381 words / 230 wpm = 6.0 min. Both within target range.

check_2_citations.notes: All three resources cited inline and present in References. No orphans.

deferred_pointers_found.verdict: named and deferred correctly — not defined or taught

check_3_no_forward_references.notes: Temperature appears once as a bare named-and-deferred pointer to 3.6, not taught. All other terms are either from prior topics 3.1–3.3 or this topic's permitted concept list.

findings.detail: Image reference uses './diagram.mmd' (the Mermaid source file) instead of the rendered PNG ('diagram.png'). Learner-facing artifact should reference the rendered image.

define_before_use.notes: All non-everyday terms defined on first use with bold-term / plain-meaning pattern: token (line 11), subword tokenisation (line 21), BPE (line 23), embedding table (line 25), next-token prediction (line 36), backpropagation (line 43), parameters/weights (line 48), attention mechanism (line 50), inference (line 66), prefill phase (line 70), autoregressive decoding (line 79), context window (line 87).

jargon_dump.notes: Each subsection introduces terms sequentially. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously.

concrete_first.notes: Token examples (lines 13-16), cat/mat training example (lines 40-45), bank/river attention example (lines 52-54), and tiktoken Worked Example all precede or accompany abstractions.

plain_register.notes: No hedging phrases found. Sentence length is consistently short to medium.

wall_of_text.notes: Paragraphs are 4-6 sentences. Step-by-step processes rendered as numbered lists (lines 38-45, 72-77).

unlisted_sequence.notes: Training steps and decode loop rendered as numbered lists, not prose.

no_inline_examples.notes: Inline examples present in Key Concepts (token split examples, BPE, bank/river). Worked Example section provides additional tiktoken code example.

bold_term_definitions.notes: Bold-term / plain-meaning pattern used consistently for all defined terms.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due-week mentions, or grade weights found in the reading.

scope_discipline.notes: Reading covers only tokens, training (next-token prediction, parameters, attention, backpropagation), and inference (prefill, decode, context window) — exactly the headline scope. Transformer architecture is referenced but not re-taught (correctly deferred to 3.2). Temperature is named-and-deferred to 3.6.

check_4_beginner_readability_formatting_.escalation_threshold: hard fail if scope_creep found OR checks 5-6 together produce >=3 distinct findings

sampled_assertions.claim: A token can be a whole word, part of a word, a punctuation mark, or even a space
BPE repeatedly merges the most frequent pairs of characters or subwords until the desired vocabulary size is reached
Embedding table is a large lookup chart that converts the integer ID into a list of numbers that encodes the token's meaning
Next-token prediction: given all the tokens seen so far, predict the most likely next token
Parameters (also called weights) are the millions or billions of numbers stored inside the neural network
Attention mechanism example with 'The bank by the river was flooded' disambiguating bank meaning
The prefill phase is relatively fast because all input tokens are processed in parallel
Autoregressive decoding — 'auto' because each new token feeds back in as input, 'regressive' because it looks backward
If a conversation grows longer than the context window, the oldest tokens fall off the edge and the model can no longer refer to them
Non-English version produces three to five times as many tokens as the English version

check_5_faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions traced directly to corpus. No hallucinated claims detected.

soft_findings_summary.detail: Image reference uses './diagram.mmd' instead of rendered './diagram.png'. Should be corrected before final delivery but does not block critic pass.
